heading: Initiation of removal proceedings
label: 1229 Initiation of removal proceedings

Consideration of Military Service in Removal Determinations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–92, div. A, title V, § 570B(b)133 Stat. 1399Dec. 20, 2019“(1).—In generalWith regards to an individual, an immigration officer shall take into consideration evidence of military service by that individual in determining whether—“(A)to issue to that individual a notice to appear in removal proceedings, an administrative order of removal, or a reinstatement of a final removal order; and“(B)to execute a final order of removal regarding that individual.“(2).—DefinitionsIn this subsection:“(A)The term ‘evidence of service’ means evidence that an individual served as a member of the Armed Forces, and the characterization of each period of service of that individual in the Armed Forces.“(B)The term ‘immigration officer’ has the meaning given that term in section 101 of the Immigration and Nationality Act ( et seq.).”8 U.S.C. 1101

A, title V, § 570B(b)133 Stat. 1399Dec. 20, 2019“(1).—In generalWith regards to an individual, an immigration officer shall take into consideration evidence of military service by that individual in determining whether—“(A)to issue to that individual a notice to appear in removal proceedings, an administrative order of removal, or a reinstatement of a final removal order; and“(B)to execute a final order of removal regarding that individual.“(2).—DefinitionsIn this subsection:“(A)The term ‘evidence of service’ means evidence that an individual served as a member of the Armed Forces, and the characterization of each period of service of that individual in the Armed Forces.“(B)The term ‘immigration officer’ has the meaning given that term in section 101 of the Immigration and Nationality Act ( et seq.).”8 U.S.C. 1101

children.label: A
children.text: The nature of the proceedings against the alien.

children.label: B
children.text: The legal authority under which the proceedings are conducted.

children.label: C
children.text: The acts or conduct alleged to be in violation of law.

children.label: D
children.text: The charges against the alien and the statutory provisions alleged to have been violated.

children.label: E
children.text: The alien may be represented by counsel and the alien will be provided (i) a period of time to secure counsel under subsection (b)(1) and (ii) a current list of counsel prepared under subsection (b)(2).

children.label: i
children.text: The requirement that the alien must immediately provide (or have provided) the Attorney General with a written record of an address and telephone number (if any) at which the alien may be contacted respecting proceedings under .section 1229a of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The requirement that the alien must provide the Attorney General immediately with a written record of any change of the alien’s address or telephone number.

children.label: iii
children.text: The consequences under of failure to provide address and telephone information pursuant to this subparagraph.section 1229a(b)(5) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: The time and place at which the proceedings will be held.

children.label: ii
children.text: The consequences under of the failure, except under exceptional circumstances, to appear at such proceedings.section 1229a(b)(5) of this title

children.heading: Notice of change in time or place of proceedings
children.label: 2 Notice of change in time or place of proceedings
i
children.text: the new time or place of the proceedings, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the consequences under of failing, except under exceptional circumstances, to attend such proceedings.section 1229a(b)(5) of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: In the case of an alien not in detention, a written notice shall not be required under this paragraph if the alien has failed to provide the address required under paragraph (1)(F).

children.heading: Central address files
children.label: 3 Central address files
children.text: The Attorney General shall create a system to record and preserve on a timely basis notices of addresses and telephone numbers (and changes) provided under paragraph (1)(F).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In order that an alien be permitted the opportunity to secure counsel before the first hearing date in proceedings under , the hearing date shall not be scheduled earlier than 10 days after the service of the notice to appear, unless the alien requests in writing an earlier hearing date.section 1229a of this title

children.heading: Current lists of counsel
children.label: 2 Current lists of counsel
children.text: The Attorney General shall provide for lists (updated not less often than quarterly) of persons who have indicated their availability to represent pro bono aliens in proceedings under . Such lists shall be provided under subsection (a)(1)(E) and otherwise made generally available.section 1229a of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection may be construed to prevent the Attorney General from proceeding against an alien pursuant to if the time period described in paragraph (1) has elapsed and the alien has failed to secure counsel.section 1229a of this title

children.heading: Service by mail
children.label: c Service by mail
children.text: Service by mail under this section shall be sufficient if there is proof of attempted delivery to the last address provided by the alien in accordance with subsection (a)(1)(F).

In the case of an alien who is convicted of an offense which makes the alien deportable, the Attorney General shall begin any removal proceeding as expeditiously as possible after the date of the conviction.

Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to create any substantive or procedural right or benefit that is legally enforceable by any party against the United States or its agencies or officers or any other person.

children.heading: Certification of compliance with restrictions on disclosure
children.label: e Certification of compliance with restrictions on disclosure

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In cases where an enforcement action leading to a removal proceeding was taken against an alien at any of the locations specified in paragraph (2), the Notice to Appear shall include a statement that the provisions of have been complied with.section 1367 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: At a domestic violence shelter, a rape crisis center, supervised visitation center, family justice center, a victim services, or victim services provider, or a community-based organization.

children.label: B
children.text: At a courthouse (or in connection with that appearance of the alien at a courthouse) if the alien is appearing in connection with a protection order case, child custody case, or other civil or criminal case relating to domestic violence, sexual assault, trafficking, or stalking in which the alien has been battered or subject to extreme cruelty or if the alien is described in subparagraph (T) or (U) of .section 1101(a)(15) of this title
